title: Route Operations
description: Implement loop-first trade route operations using Knot hierarchy. Initial route must be A->B->A, then support Pipe/Grow/Collapse with preview, confirm, and undo. Current backend operation logic and operation records are implemented; current blocker is RoutePlanningTool toolbar and operation overlay rendering visibility in TradeScene.
definition-of-done: Pipe and Grow produce a single continuous loop and preserve intended hierarchy behavior. RoutePlanningTool shows visible tool UI and operation preview/selection feedback. Undo/redo correctly reverses and reapplies each Pipe/Grow operation record. User can confirm operations via Enter or UI control.
testing-plan: Create initial route from HQ + city and verify A->B->A loop. Add singleton city via Grow defaults and confirm edge replacement behavior. Pipe two non-singleton knots and verify edge cuts/adds plus hierarchy behavior. Validate undo/redo sequence for Pipe/Grow operations. Verify toolbar/hover/confirm UI visibility and interaction in TradeScene.
todos: DONE : Implement operation record system (OperationRecord, PipeRecord, GrowRecord, CollapseRecord) and route operation stack
DONE : Implement loop-first Pipe for singleton + singleton (A->B->A)
DONE : Implement Grow for singleton insertion into selected/default lowest-cost edge
DONE : Implement Pipe for knot + knot with edge selection, hierarchy-aware behavior, and preview defaults
DONE : Implement Collapse path conversion logic and record-based undo/redo
DONE : Remove road-connectivity restriction from route construction flow
DONE : Add keybind handling and routing through TradeKeyGuy to RoutePlanningTool
DONE : Fix RoutePlanningTool toolbar and SDF overlay rendering visibility in TradeScene
DONE : Re-enable and polish tooltips/button labels/hover feedback once toolbar is visible
Complete end-to-end validation for Pipe/Grow including hierarchy navigation
unknowns: Why RoutePlanningTool SDF overlay draws are not visible while city rendering remains visible in TradeScene
Whether toolbar/operation UI should render in a dedicated HUD view (separate camera view bounds) instead of VIEW_MAIN

pipe.current-status: Backend logic implemented for singleton-singleton, singleton-knot (grow-like), and knot-knot with edge selection and hierarchy-aware routing.
design-intent: Pipe is the route-combine operation. Routes are always loops, never open chains.
Singleton + singleton: immediate 2-point loop A->B->A with no edge cuts.
Knot(2+) + singleton: behaves as Grow (cut one edge on knot, insert singleton, add two edges).
Knot(2+) + knot(2+): cut one edge on each knot, add two cross-edges, preserve one continuous loop.
Default selected cut edges should be lowest-cost, but player can rotate/change edge choice before confirm.
When piping between knot-level structures, operation should create or reuse the appropriate higher-order knot based on selected edge type (sub-knot edge vs pipe edge).
ui-flow: Player clicks first knot/city, then second knot/city.
System enters PREVIEW with default lowest-cost cut/add edge set.
Player can click alternative edges on either knot to rotate the plan.
Confirm via Enter or confirm button to commit recordable operation.
next-steps: Validate edge rotation/change selection UX in PREVIEW before confirm.
Add explicit visual indicators for selected cut edges and resulting add edges once overlay render issue is fixed.
Run manual scenarios for same-level pipe vs new-hierarchy pipe and verify operation records.

grow.current-status: Grow insertion and lowest-cost default edge selection are implemented; one-click add flow after initial route exists is wired.
design-intent: Grow modifies an existing loop in-place; it does not create a new hierarchy level by itself.
Operation cuts exactly one existing edge and inserts one singleton city into that edge.
Net topology change is remove one edge, add two edges, keeping the route a single loop.
Roads do not gate whether a city can be added; roads only affect efficiency/scoring later.
Default insertion edge is lowest-cost, but player can select a different edge before confirming.
ui-flow: Player selects a route knot/city, then selects an out-of-route singleton city.
System previews split edge (remove) and two new edges (add).
Player can rotate/change insertion edge in preview.
Confirm applies grow and records undo/redo data.
next-steps: Confirm preview consistently appears for route city + singleton city selection across all states.
Add clear UI copy for default insertion edge and alternate edge selection behavior.
Validate undo/redo consistency when multiple grows are chained.

collapse.current-status: Core collapse transformation and record model are implemented for linked hierarchy paths.
design-intent: Collapse is the path-compression operation between two knots in the same hierarchy chain.
Goal is single-loop preservation while converting double-pipe style links into a single-pipe/crossing structure.
For endpoints (e.g., C1 and C5), remove one segment from each endpoint-side pipe pairing and add direct C1<->C5 connection.
Across intermediate knots on the selected chain, convert paired pipe links by removing one of each pair and adding one internal crossing segment per knot to maintain continuity.
Result must not create inside/outside dual loops; invariant is one continuous traversable loop.
Collapse should produce a higher-order representation capturing the transformed relationship and support full undo.
ui-flow: Player selects start knot and end knot in same hierarchy context.
System finds valid path and shows default segments to remove/add.
Preview highlights removed pipe segments, added internal crossings, and new endpoint connection.
Confirm commits collapse as a single operation record.
next-steps: Add stronger visual preview of removed pipe segments and internal crossing segments before confirm.
Stress test on 4-8 knot chains to verify single-loop invariant after collapse.
Validate hierarchy parent/child updates and navigation behavior after collapse and undo.
